# Trust & Graduated Autonomy

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)**

## The Commitment

Autonomy is earned, never assumed. Every entity — human, agent, process, or
venture — starts with constrained authority and earns expanded authority through
demonstrated competence. Trust moves in both directions: earned through success,
revoked through failure.

## What This Means

- New entities begin at the lowest appropriate oversight level.
- Trust increases are based on observable evidence (successful execution, quality of output, adherence to governance).
- Trust decreases are triggered by failures, anomalies, or governance violations.
- The oversight level for any entity is always inspectable.
- No entity reaches full autonomy — the highest level still includes periodic human audit.

## Violation Looks Like

An agent is deployed with full autonomy on day one because "we trust the model."
A venture is given access to shared resources without demonstrating governance compliance.
A process bypasses approval gates because "it worked last time."

## Derived From

- JOS-C16 (Trust Spectrum)
